description: Closure contract split across Eval, P0 bytecode, and target runtime. Known-open bytecode rows may pin the expected compiler diagnostic while closure objects are absent.

cases.notes: P0 lowers the immediate lambda into the surrounding lexical scope; no first-class closure object is created.
Shadowing is green where the lambda can be lowered or compiled as a noncapturing helper.
The lambda has no captured variables and can be represented as a helper symbol.

cases.expect_bytecode_error: capturing closure is not supported in P0
cases.open_issue: P0 has helper functions but no environment object for returned capturing closures.

cases.expect_bytecode_error: capturing closure is not supported in P0
cases.open_issue: Nested closure return requires heap-allocated environment capture.

cases.notes: Variadic helper functions are already represented in P0 code-object flags.

cases.expect_bytecode_error: capturing closure is not supported in P0
cases.open_issue: &rest works for helpers, but captured x needs a closure environment.

cases.expect_bytecode_error: capturing closure is not supported in P0
cases.open_issue: Tail-position funcall of a capturing closure requires both closure objects and a target TCO policy.
